hero.title: Find Your Balance. Live with Purpose.
hero.subtitle: Discover practices for inner peace, well-being, and a fulfilled life — grounded in science, open to all.
wisdom.title: The Path to Well-Being
wisdom.p1: Well-being is not a destination — it's a practice. Every day, we have the opportunity to nurture our mind, body, and spirit through simple, proven techniques that humanity has refined over millennia.
wisdom.p2: WellBeingBar brings together 108 practices from secular mindfulness, positive psychology, Stoic philosophy, nature wisdom, and modern neuroscience. Each one is rated for scientific evidence so you can make informed choices.
wisdom.p3: There's no one-size-fits-all path. Explore, experiment, and discover what works for you.

tradition.mindfulness_secular.desc: Rooted in ancient contemplative traditions but stripped of religious context, secular mindfulness trains your attention to stay present. Purpose: cultivate non-judgmental awareness of thoughts, emotions, and sensations. Results: reduced stress and anxiety, sharper focus, better emotional regulation, and a calmer, more centered mind.
tradition.stoicism.desc: Founded in ancient Greece by Zeno of Citium and refined by Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and Epictetus, Stoicism teaches that inner peace comes from mastering your response to life — not from controlling events. Purpose: build resilience, clarity, and virtue through rational self-examination. Results: greater emotional stability, less reactivity to setbacks, stronger sense of purpose, and a life guided by wisdom rather than impulse.
tradition.positive_psychology.desc: Launched by Martin Seligman in the late 1990s, positive psychology shifted the focus of science from fixing what's broken to understanding what makes life worth living. Purpose: identify and strengthen the habits, mindsets, and relationships that lead to lasting well-being. Results: increased happiness and life satisfaction, stronger resilience, deeper gratitude, more flow experiences, and healthier social bonds.
tradition.eastern_movement.desc: Spanning thousands of years across India, China, and Japan, these practices — yoga, tai chi, qigong, and martial arts — unite body and mind through intentional movement and breath. Purpose: harmonize physical energy, calm the nervous system, and develop body awareness. Results: improved flexibility and balance, reduced chronic pain, lower blood pressure, deeper relaxation, and a profound sense of embodied presence.
tradition.nature_wisdom.desc: Drawing from indigenous knowledge, Japanese forest bathing (shinrin-yoku), and ecological science, nature wisdom recognizes that humans heal and thrive when connected to the natural world. Purpose: restore the bond between people and their environment through outdoor practices. Results: lower cortisol and stress hormones, boosted immunity, improved mood and creativity, and a renewed sense of wonder and belonging.
tradition.modern_science.desc: Built on neuroscience, clinical psychology, sleep research, and physiology, these practices are designed and tested in laboratories before reaching your daily routine. Purpose: apply evidence-based techniques to optimize mental health, physical recovery, and cognitive performance. Results: better sleep quality, improved brain function, stronger immune response, measurable reductions in anxiety and depression, and habits grounded in peer-reviewed research.
about.title: About WellBeingBar
about.intro: WellBeingBar is a free knowledge platform dedicated to helping people discover practices for inner peace, well-being, and a fulfilled life. We believe that everyone deserves access to scientifically grounded information about practices that can improve their quality of life.
about.mission.title: Our Mission
about.mission.text: We provide clear, science-backed information about well-being practices from diverse traditions — without dogma, without judgment, and without pushing any particular belief system. You explore, you choose what resonates with you.
about.philosophy.title: Our Philosophy
about.philosophy.text: Life has ups and downs — that is natural and beautiful. We don't promise to eliminate all negativity. Instead, we offer tools and knowledge to help you build resilience, find balance, and embrace life fully. True well-being isn't about being happy all the time — it's about having the inner resources to navigate whatever comes your way.
about.approach.title: Our Approach
about.approach.text: Every practice on WellBeingBar is rated for scientific evidence, making it easy to distinguish well-researched techniques from emerging ones. We draw from secular mindfulness, positive psychology, Stoic philosophy, nature wisdom, and modern neuroscience — always respecting the origins while keeping the content accessible and non-religious.
about.open.title: Free & Open
about.open.text: WellBeingBar is free to use, with no ads, no tracking cookies, and no data collection. We believe knowledge about well-being should be accessible to everyone.
methodology.title: Data Sources & Methodology
methodology.science.title: Scientific Sources
methodology.academic.title: Academic & Institutional
methodology.rating.title: Evidence Rating System
methodology.rating.text: Each practice is rated on a 1-5 scale for scientific evidence: (1) Anecdotal, (2) Preliminary studies, (3) Clinical trials, (4) Strong evidence, (5) Extensive meta-analyses.
methodology.disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
methodology.disclaimer.text: WellBeingBar provides informational content only and is not a substitute for professional medical or psychological advice. If you are experiencing mental health challenges, please consult a qualified healthcare professional.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.overview.title: Overview
privacy.overview.text: WellBeingBar respects your privacy. We do not use cookies for tracking, we do not collect personal data, and we do not share any information with third parties.
privacy.analytics.title: Analytics
privacy.analytics.text: We use GoatCounter, a privacy-friendly analytics tool that does not use cookies and does not collect personal data.
privacy.storage.title: Local Storage
privacy.storage.text: We use your browser's local storage only to remember your language preference. This data stays on your device.
privacy.hosting.title: Hosting
privacy.hosting.text: This website is hosted on GitHub Pages. Please refer to GitHub's privacy statement for details.
impressum.title: Legal Notice (Impressum)
impressum.provider.name: WellBeingBar
impressum.responsible.title: Responsible for Content
impressum.responsible.text: WellBeingBar is a free, non-commercial knowledge platform. All content is provided for informational purposes only.
impressum.liability.title: Liability
impressum.liability.text: Despite careful content control, we assume no liability for the content of external links.

principles.title: Guiding Principles
principles.subtitle: What it means to be good, to do good, and to live a fulfilled life
principles.intro_p1: Across all cultures, traditions, and centuries, certain truths about living well keep appearing. These are not commandments or rules — they are gentle guideposts, discovered again and again by those who sought a meaningful life.
principles.intro_p2: These principles are not about perfection. They are about direction. Every small step toward kindness, presence, or purpose adds up to a life well lived.
principles.kindness.title: Practice Kindness
principles.kindness.text: Being good starts with how we treat others. Small acts of kindness — a genuine smile, a helping hand, a patient ear — create ripples that transform both giver and receiver. Kindness is not weakness; it is the quiet strength that holds communities together.
principles.kindness.quote: "No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted." — Aesop
principles.gratitude.title: Cultivate Gratitude
principles.gratitude.text: A grateful heart sees abundance where others see lack. Taking time to notice what is good — your health, a friend's laugh, a warm meal — rewires the brain for happiness. Gratitude turns what we have into enough.
principles.gratitude.quote: "When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive." — Marcus Aurelius
principles.presence.title: Live in the Present
principles.presence.text: The past is memory, the future is imagination — the only moment you can truly live is now. When you are fully present, worry dissolves, beauty reveals itself, and life becomes vivid. This is where peace lives.
principles.presence.quote: "The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it." — Thich Nhat Hanh
principles.growth.title: Never Stop Growing
principles.growth.text: A fulfilled life is one of continuous learning and becoming. Growth doesn't mean always achieving more — it means deepening your understanding, expanding your compassion, and becoming more authentically yourself with each passing year.
principles.growth.quote: "The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance." — Alan Watts
principles.service.title: Serve Others
principles.service.text: Doing good means looking beyond yourself. The deepest satisfaction comes not from what we accumulate but from what we give. Service — whether to family, community, or strangers — connects us to something larger than our own story.
principles.service.quote: "The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others." — Mahatma Gandhi
principles.balance.title: Seek Balance
principles.balance.text: Well-being is not about extremes. It is the art of balance — between effort and rest, solitude and connection, discipline and spontaneity. Like a tree that bends with the wind, a balanced life is flexible, rooted, and resilient.
principles.balance.quote: "Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished." — Lao Tzu
principles.purpose.title: Find Your Purpose
principles.purpose.text: A life with purpose is a life with direction. Purpose doesn't have to be grand — it can be raising a family well, creating beauty, helping one person each day. What matters is that your daily actions align with what you truly value.
principles.purpose.quote: "He who has a why to live can bear almost any how." — Friedrich Nietzsche
principles.forgiveness.title: Practice Forgiveness
principles.forgiveness.text: Holding onto resentment is like carrying a heavy stone — it only burdens the one who carries it. Forgiveness is not about condoning what happened; it is about freeing yourself. When you forgive, you reclaim your peace.
principles.forgiveness.quote: "Forgiveness is the fragrance that the violet sheds on the heel that has crushed it." — Mark Twain
principles.acceptance.title: Embrace What Is
principles.acceptance.text: Life has ups and downs — that is its nature. Acceptance doesn't mean giving up; it means acknowledging reality as the starting point for change. When you stop fighting what is, energy becomes available for what can be.
principles.acceptance.quote: "We suffer more often in imagination than in reality." — Seneca
principles.integrity.title: Live with Integrity
principles.integrity.text: Being good means being the same person in private as you are in public. Integrity is the alignment between your values and your actions. It builds trust — with others and with yourself. A clear conscience is the softest pillow.
principles.integrity.quote: "The unexamined life is not worth living." — Socrates
principles.resilience.title: Build Resilience
principles.resilience.text: Life will knock you down. What defines you is not the fall but how you rise. Resilience is not about avoiding pain — it's about developing the inner resources to navigate difficulty with grace, learning from setbacks, and growing stronger through challenges.
principles.resilience.quote: "Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our freedom." — Viktor Frankl
principles.connection.title: Nurture Connection
principles.connection.text: We are social beings woven into the fabric of life. Deep, authentic relationships — with family, friends, community, and nature — are the greatest predictor of happiness. Connection is not about the number of people you know, but the depth of your bonds.
principles.connection.quote: "We're all just walking each other home." — Ram Dass
principles.closing_text: There is no finish line. Well-being is not a place you arrive at — it is how you walk the path. Some days you will stumble, some days you will soar. What matters is that you keep walking, keep growing, and keep your heart open. The very act of seeking a good life is already living one.
